title: Expressing support for the designation of May 2025 as "National Asthma and Allergy Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to make May 2025 a special month to teach people about asthma and allergies. The bill explains that millions of Americans have asthma and allergies, and these health problems affect kids and some racial groups more than others. These diseases cause people to miss school and work, and they cost our country billions of dollars. The House of Representatives wants all Americans to do activities during this month to learn more about asthma and allergies.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for making May 2025 National Asthma and Allergy Awareness Month.

sections.title: Facts About Asthma and Allergies
sections.text: Four million nine hundred thousand children in the United States currently have asthma. Nearly twenty eight million people in the United States have asthma and the numbers are growing each year. In two thousand eighteen, children on average missed more than seven million nine hundred thousand days of school and adults missed ten million nine hundred thousand days of work because of asthma. Asthma is one of the most common diseases that do not spread from person to person among children. One million five hundred thousand people per year visit emergency rooms because of asthma problems. Asthma is a leading cause of hospital stays and missed school days due to ongoing illness among children. The total cost to the economy from asthma in the United States was eighty one billion nine hundred million dollars per year from two thousand eight to two thousand thirteen. Fewer than half of people with asthma report being given an asthma action plan, which is important for preventing asthma attacks. Black, Hispanic, and Indigenous people in the United States face the highest burden of asthma. Asthma is a disease that can be managed but it still caused the deaths of over three thousand five hundred Americans in two thousand twenty one. Twenty million three hundred thousand people in the United States have food allergies, including about four million three hundred thousand children. Food allergies have increased among United States children over the past twenty years, with the greatest increase in Black children. Children with food allergies are two to four times more likely to have asthma or other allergic diseases. May would be a good month to designate as Asthma and Allergy Awareness Month.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives recognizes National Asthma and Allergy Awareness Month and calls upon the American people to observe this month with appropriate ceremonies and activities.
